title: Light Malaysian Fish Bun
description: A delicate and flavorful Malaysian-inspired dish featuring steamed fish and soft buns, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This pescatarian-friendly recipe combines the freshness of fish with the comfort of a warm bun.

ingredients.name: fish fillets
ingredients.notes: any white fish works, such as cod or tilapia

instructions: Prepare the steamer by filling the bottom with 1 cup of water and bringing it to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and place the fish fillets in the steamer basket.
Steam the fish for 8-10 minutes or until cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, toast the buns by steaming them for 2-3 minutes.
Prepare the sauce by mixing soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, pepper, and lemon juice in a small bowl.
Once the fish is cooked, remove it from the steamer and place it on a plate.
Brush the sauce over the fish and garnish with chopped scallions and sliced ginger.
Serve the fish with the toasted buns and enjoy.
You can also add other toppings such as sliced cucumbers or pickled carrots to the buns.
To assemble, place a piece of fish on each bun and add your desired toppings.
